headline: Microsoft, Europol, and partners ran Operation Endgame to dismantle the StealC, Amadey, and SocGholish cybercrime services, recovering 27 million stolen credentials in a strike on the criminal supply chain.

emerging_trends.text: Coordinated takedowns and courtroom wins dominated the day, as Operation Endgame gutted infostealer infrastructure while courts jailed a DraftKings hacker and the Justice Department seized scam-laundering accounts tied to Cambodia's Prince Group.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Operation Endgame Dismantles StealC, Amadey, and SocGholish

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep racing patch cycles on network gear, exploiting a Cisco SD-WAN zero-day for root access two months before its disclosure and weaponizing fresh flaws in Cisco Unified Communications Manager.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Cisco SD-WAN and Unified CM Flaws Exploited

emerging_trends.text: Threat actors increasingly turn trusted system features against defenders, chaining macOS behaviors to switch off endpoint protection without admin rights and abusing Microsoft Edge native messaging to escape the browser sandbox.
emerging_trends.topic_title: macOS Flaws Let Users Silently Kill Security Agents

topics.title: Operation Endgame Dismantles StealC, Amadey, and SocGholish
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft, Europol, Proofpoint, Bitdefender, Bitsight, and ESET widened the operation to three cybercrime-as-a-service families—adding SocGholish alongside StealC and Amadey—disrupted more than 300 command-and-control servers, and recovered 27 million stolen credentials, with Microsoft's Digital Crimes Unit framing the sweep as a strike on the full cybercrime supply chain.
topics.summary: StealC steals credentials and Amadey loads follow-on payloads; together the families compromise devices, harvest data, and stage ransomware and fraud. Organizations should rotate exposed credentials and hunt for the malware on their networks.

sources.title: One-two punch delivered in global operation disrupts cybercrime "assembly line"
Three 'cybercrime as a service' operations undercut by Microsoft, law enforcement
Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
StealC and Amadey: Breaking down infostealers and the cybercrime services that deliver them

topics.title: Cisco SD-WAN and Unified CM Flaws Exploited
topics.latest_developments: Mandiant disclosed that attackers exploited CVE-2026-20245 in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN as a zero-day two months before its disclosure, using rogue peering to reach devices and forge root-level accounts, while honeypots caught automated Tor sweeps dropping webshells through Unified Communications Manager flaw CVE-2026-20230 after a proof-of-concept exposed a file-write path to root.
topics.summary: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN and Unified Communications Manager run enterprise networking and voice systems; both flaws hand unauthenticated attackers code execution and root. Administrators should patch and inspect for rogue accounts and webshells.

sources.title: Mandiant reveals how Cisco SD-WAN zero-day attacks gained root access
Attackers Hit Cisco SD-WAN Flaw 2 Months Before Disclosure
Cisco Unified CM flaw actively exploited to drop webshells (CVE-2026-20230)
Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root

topics.title: Courts and Treasury Squeeze Cybercrime Operators
topics.latest_developments: A U.S. court sentenced Nathan Austad, alias Snoopy, to 18 months and roughly $1.8 million for the November 2022 DraftKings account takeovers; Spain extradited an Algerian national who ran phishing-kit and fraud-tool marketplaces to face bank fraud charges; and the Justice Department seized a HuiOne Group cloud account as the Treasury sanctioned nine individuals and 26 entities tied to Cambodia's Prince Group for laundering scam proceeds.
topics.summary: The actions span credential-stuffing theft, phishing-kit sales, and Cambodian scam laundering, applying sustained pressure on cybercrime operators and the networks that move their cash.

sources.title: Third DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to 18 Months in Prison
Algerian national accused of running cybercrime marketplaces extradited to US
DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering

topics.title: macOS Flaws Let Users Silently Kill Security Agents
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek and Dark Reading detailed a chain of macOS weaknesses that lets a standard non-admin account disable endpoint security and integrated browser tools by abusing legitimate operating-system behavior, sidestepping administrator privileges and kernel exploits entirely.
topics.summary: The technique blinds EDR and browser protections on Macs without elevated rights, leaving fleets that rely on those agents exposed. Defenders should monitor for security tooling that goes dark.

sources.title: macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents
Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools

topics.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Draws a Surge in Cyber Threats
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading flagged a surge in cybercrime, social engineering, and infrastructure threats targeting the tournament across the United States, Canada, and Mexico, while researcher Bob DaHacker revealed she had obtained the live broadcast controls that could have Rickrolled the entire event.
topics.summary: The 2026 World Cup, which the United States, Canada, and Mexico co-host, presents a sprawling attack surface across venues, broadcast systems, and fans. Organizers and sponsors face phishing, fraud, and infrastructure risk.

sources.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Faces Surge in Cyber Threats
Smashing Security podcast #473: How a hacker could have Rickrolled the entire World Cup

topics.title: Ransomware Strikes Bajaj Auto as Hackers Loot Madison Square Garden
topics.latest_developments: Bajaj Auto, India's motorcycle giant, told regulators it discovered a ransomware incident Tuesday morning and moved to contain it, while separate hackers stole more than 45 gigabytes from Madison Square Garden, including data on the New York Knicks and the venue's talent.
topics.summary: Both incidents hit marquee organizations—an Indian manufacturer and a New York entertainment landmark—through ransomware and data theft, threatening operational disruption and the exposure of sensitive records.

sources.title: Indian auto giant Bajaj Auto hit by ransomware incident
How Hackers Broke into Madison Square Garden

weather: Thursday: Partly Sunny then Scattered Showers And Thunderstorms, high 84F.
Thursday Night: Scattered Showers And Thunderstorms then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 63F.

games.recap: Cole Young hits a two-run home run in the seventh to lift Mariners to a 3-2 victory over Pirates

games.result: Mariners 1 · Pirates 10 · Bot 8th (in progress at last update)

business_politics.title: Banks Pass Fed Stress Test, Lift Payouts
business_politics.latest_developments: The Federal Reserve released its 2026 stress-test results Wednesday, and several Wall Street lenders immediately raised dividends and buybacks.
business_politics.summary: The Fed's annual test found the largest U.S. banks would absorb roughly $700 billion in losses in a severe economic crash yet stay above capital minimums; JPMorgan and Bank of America passed comfortably under a methodology the Fed eased by publishing test details in advance, and lenders responded by boosting investor payouts.

sources.title: US banks would lose $700bn in economic crash, Fed stress tests find
Big Banks Ace an Easier Annual Stress Test

business_politics.title: Trump Slams NATO, Seeks $88 Billion From Congress
business_politics.latest_developments: A day after canceling the housing-bill signing, President Trump demanded 'loyalty' from NATO allies over the Iran war, and the White House formally asked Congress for $87.6 billion, mostly to replenish the Pentagon.
business_politics.summary: After meeting Secretary-General Mark Rutte, Trump publicly singled out alliance members including the United Kingdom for failing to back the U.S. campaign against Iran, while his administration sent Congress an $87.6 billion supplemental request covering war costs, aid to U.S. farmers, and an Ebola response, landing amid a shouting match with Republican lawmakers.

sources.title: Trump demands ‘loyalty’ from Nato allies and slams lack of support in Iran war
White House seeks $88bn from Congress as Trump clashes with Republicans

business.title: City Heading for $24 Million Deficit
business.latest_developments: Pittsburgh budget officials told a Wednesday finance discussion the city could finish 2026 about $24 million in the red even after this year's tax increase and spending cuts.
business.summary: Pittsburgh raised taxes and trimmed spending heading into 2026, yet city finance officials now project a roughly $24 million year-end deficit, deepening the budget pressure facing City Council.

sources.title: Pittsburgh on pace to end year with $24M deficit despite tax hike, cuts

business.title: Precision Care Pharmacy Closes in Greensburg
business.latest_developments: Precision Care Pharmacy in Greensburg has sat shuttered for a week, leaving former customers without their prescriptions or an explanation.
business.summary: Precision Care Pharmacy, a Greensburg pharmacy in Westmoreland County, abruptly closed and has stayed dark for a week, frustrating customers who say the business never told them where to transfer their prescriptions.

sources.title: Customers furious, left without answers after Greensburg pharmacy suddenly shuts down

business.title: Strip District Grocer Expands as Pizzeria Pauses
business.latest_developments: Rockaway Pizzeria has temporarily closed, Mayfly Market is expanding into the Strip District, and Leon's Caribbean has left Allentown, TribLive reported Wednesday.
business.summary: Pittsburgh's food scene shifted this week: Rockaway Pizzeria shut its doors temporarily, the grocer Mayfly Market announced an expansion to the Strip District, and the longtime Leon's Caribbean departed the Allentown neighborhood.

sources.title: Rockaway Pizzeria temporarily closes, Mayfly Market expands, Leon’s Carribean leaves Allentown

around_town.title: Realtor Flags 35 Deed-Fraud Cases
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh real estate agent Kelsey Green said she has documented 35 cases of deed fraud across Allegheny County, most in the city and concentrated on the South Side.
around_town.summary: Kelsey Green, a Pittsburgh real estate agent, says she uncovered 35 properties whose deeds were fraudulently transferred away from owners current on their mortgages, with the bulk of the cases in the South Side neighborhood.

sources.title: Pittsburgh real estate agent says she's uncovered dozens of cases of deed fraud

around_town.title: Measles Spreads to Northumberland County
around_town.latest_developments: Pennsylvania's measles count jumped and the outbreak reached Northumberland County, the Post-Gazette reported Wednesday.
around_town.summary: Pennsylvania recorded a rise in measles cases that has now spread to Northumberland County, widening a public-health concern across the state.

sources.title: Pa. measles cases jump, spread to Northumberland County

around_town.title: Turnpike Braces for America 250 Traffic
around_town.latest_developments: The Pennsylvania Turnpike released its America 250 traffic forecast Wednesday, warning of heavier-than-usual volume around the nation's 250th-anniversary celebrations.
around_town.summary: The Pennsylvania Turnpike Commission projected unusually heavy traffic on the state's largest toll road as travelers move for the country's 250th-birthday events, and it laid out construction and travel updates for the holiday stretch.

sources.title: Pa. Turnpike releases America 250 traffic forecast and updates

events.title: Warhol Bridge Turns 100; OpenStreetPGH Returns
events.latest_developments: NEXTpittsburgh's June 25-28 guide leads with the Andy Warhol Bridge 100th Birthday Bash and OpenStreetPGH.
events.summary: Pittsburgh's weekend of Thursday through Sunday, June 25 to 28, features the Andy Warhol Bridge 100th Birthday Bash, marking a century of the downtown span, and OpenStreetPGH, which closes city streets to cars, among more than a dozen listed events.

sources.title: 14+ things to do this weekend, from the Andy Warhol Bridge 100th Birthday Bash to OpenStreetPGH

around_teams.title: Steelers Add Cornerback Jamel Dean
around_teams.latest_developments: Steelers.com's SNR Drive broke down the addition of cornerback Jamel Dean to Pittsburgh's defense, with Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler weighing his fit.
around_teams.summary: Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler used the Steelers' SNR Drive to assess cornerback Jamel Dean's arrival, gauging how the veteran slots into the secondary for 2026.

sources.title: SNR Drive talks on the addition of Jamel Dean on the Steelers defense

around_teams.title: Is the NFL's Priciest Defense Delivering?
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette video asked whether the Steelers are getting enough production from the NFL's most expensive defense, anchored by T.J. Watt and Cam Heyward.
around_teams.summary: Post-Gazette analysts questioned the return on the Steelers' league-high defensive payroll, which carries T.J. Watt and Cam Heyward, as the team heads toward the 2026 season.

sources.title: WATCH: Are Steelers getting enough from NFL's most expensive defense?

around_teams.title: Wietgrefe's Scoreless Streak in Altoona
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette's MiLB Monday spotlighted Pirates prospect Connor Wietgrefe, who has strung together a scoreless streak at Double-A Altoona.
around_teams.summary: Pirates pitching prospect Connor Wietgrefe is drawing notice for a scoreless streak with the Double-A Altoona Curve, the Post-Gazette reported in its minor-league roundup.

sources.title: MiLB Monday: Pirates prospect Connor Wietgrefe is turning heads with his scoreless streak in Altoona

team_usa.title: Pochettino Rests Yellow-Carded Starters vs Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: Mauricio Pochettino said Wednesday he will not start his four yellow-carded players against Türkiye on Thursday, June 25, to keep them eligible for the round of 32 on July 1.
team_usa.summary: With the U.S. men already through to the knockouts as a World Cup co-host, manager Mauricio Pochettino will hold out the four players carrying yellow cards in Thursday's Group D finale against Türkiye, avoiding suspensions that would carry into the round-of-32 match July 1.

sources.title: Pochettino won't 'risk' USMNT players on yellows

team_usa.title: Milan Rejects NYCFC Bid for Pulisic
team_usa.latest_developments: AC Milan turned down a New York City FC approach for Christian Pulisic, telling sources the U.S. attacker is not for sale.
team_usa.summary: AC Milan rejected an inquiry from New York City FC for forward Christian Pulisic during the World Cup, with the Italian club insisting the United States attacker is not available for transfer.

sources.title: Sources: Milan rejects NYCFC approach for Pulisic

team_usa.title: Balogun Among World Cup's Top Scorers
team_usa.latest_developments: Folarin Balogun has scored twice in two World Cup matches, ranking him among the tournament's leading scorers and drawing interest from Europe's biggest clubs.
team_usa.summary: U.S. striker Folarin Balogun's two goals in two group games place him among the World Cup's top scorers and have prompted some of Europe's largest clubs to take notice.

sources.title: USMNT's Balogun has the World Cup, Europe's biggest clubs taking notice

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: My Vibe Coding Adventure, The App and the Experience, Ten Takeaways
reading.summary: Ben Thompson recounts vibe coding a working app he plans to use regularly, then draws ten takeaways about what the experience reveals about AI-assisted software development.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues much of the AI and tech industry practices a cargo cult, mimicking the rituals and rhetoric of successful companies without the underlying value or economics to justify the hype.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the “Doom Trolling”
reading.summary: Newport contends that AI firms publishing alarming warnings about the dangers of their own products amounts to a marketing tactic, as absurd as a carmaker warning its bestselling truck is too powerful to control.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
